assertions.expectation: Identifies PDB as the most likely/common cause of stuck upgrades
assertions.reason: Response explicitly identifies Pod Disruption Budgets (PDBs) as a 'Common Culprit' and provides detailed section on checking and fixing PDB issues

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl command to check PDBs (kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces or similar)
assertions.reason: Provides exact command 'kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces' and also 'kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces -o wide'

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify which pods are blocking the drain
assertions.reason: Includes multiple commands: 'kubectl get pods --all-namespaces --field-selector=status.phase=Running', 'kubectl get pods --all-namespaces | grep Terminating', and 'kubectl get pods --all-namespaces --field-selector=spec.nodeName=<stuck-node-name>'

assertions.expectation: Includes resource constraint diagnosis (capacity issues, pending pods)
assertions.reason: Does not mention capacity issues, pending pods, or resource constraints as potential causes of stuck upgrades

assertions.expectation: Provides a fix for restrictive PDBs (patch or temporary adjustment)
assertions.reason: Provides specific patch command 'kubectl patch pdb <pdb-name> -n <namespace> --type merge -p '{"spec":{"minAvailable":0}}'' and bulk fix for all PDBs

assertions.expectation: Mentions that GKE has a PDB timeout (up to 1 hour) after which it may force-drain, and that GKE sends notifications when eviction is blocked by PDB
assertions.reason: Does not mention GKE's PDB timeout behavior or automatic notifications when eviction is blocked

assertions.expectation: Includes guidance on surge upgrade settings as a potential fix
assertions.reason: Includes surge settings in the upgrade restart command: '--max-surge=1 --max-unavailable=0'

assertions.expectation: Mentions webhook issues as a possible cause
assertions.reason: Does not mention admission webhooks or webhook issues as a potential cause of stuck upgrades

assertions.expectation: Includes validation steps to confirm the fix is working
assertions.reason: Provides monitoring commands in section 6: 'watch' commands for operations and node status, plus 'kubectl get events' suggestion

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE disruption-event notifications (POD_PDB_VIOLATION) that alert when eviction is blocked by PDB, available via Cloud Logging or Pub/Sub
assertions.reason: Does not mention POD_PDB_VIOLATION notifications, Cloud Logging, or Pub/Sub for disruption events
